page: Microsoft Azure D4lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and features a massive 480 MB L3 cache, alongside 2 MB L2 cache and 48 KB L1D cache. The instance includes 236 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, but does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU test ranks in the top 25% with a score of 2290.98, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression achieve top-tier status in the top 10%. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression falling into the bottom 25%. Memory read bandwidth is average at 345.13 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 43.66 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, while large block write bandwidth is weak at 23.53 GB/sec. LLM inference on a medium 7B model is poor, but the server handles small 135M models with average efficiency.

The D4lds_v7 offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that require high clock speeds and strong single-threaded performance but do not demand large memory footprints. By pairing a high-frequency 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor with a constrained 8.0 GB of RAM, it avoids the premium costs associated with high-memory instances. This hardware balance makes it suitable for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and entry-level containerized applications that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and local disk storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D4lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance includes 236 GB of local storage and features a large 480 MB L3 cache. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, with single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reaching the top 10% tier. However, multi-core scaling and large-block memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. The low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core makes this instance a cost-effective choice for compute-heavy, single-threaded workloads that do not require massive memory arrays. It is well-suited for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and utility computing tasks.

8.0 GB of system memory and 236 GB of local storage
Strong single-core performance and top-tier single-threaded compression speeds

Best suited for lightweight web serving, basic database tasks, and single-threaded utilities
